vllm.title: Implement ARC KV cache eviction policy
vllm.author: albertoperdomo2
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Current vLLM v1 uses LRU for KV cache eviction, which works well for scan-heavy workloads but can struggle with mixed access patterns. ARC automatically adapts between recency-based (LRU-like) and frequency-based (LFU-like) eviction, providing better cache efficiency for diverse workloads.
A

On H100, DeepGEMM needs the scales to be ColumnMajor and in float32.

vllm.title: [MoE][Kernel][Perf] Improve Shared Expert Stream Overlap
vllm.author: alexm-redhat
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
SUMMARY:

use single global auxilary stream to avoid pytorch proifler stream explosion
limit parallel stream execution to <=256 batch size
remove shared expert overlap from chunked_impl (dead code)

vllm.title: Rename clashing method names for vLLM model protocol
vllm.author: hmellor
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
get_input_embeddings is a getter method of all transformers.PreTrainedModels.
This name had been reused in vLLM to call forward on the model's embedding layer.
Since the method in vLLM is not actually a getter and causes a confusing clash with the getter in Transformers, this PR:

Renames get_input_

vllm.title: [n-gen] DO NOT repeatedly return finished child requests
vllm.author: Jialin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
For non FINAL_ONLY mode, currently, finished child requests would be repeatedly returned to the clients which is confusing and misleading.
In this PR, we change the behavior to ensure each finished child request would only be returned ONCE.
Test Plan & Test Result
Add unit test to gate the l

vllm.title: [Frontend] split append tool output
vllm.author: qandrew
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
append_output currently does two things, it takes an input from the engine (as type RequestOutput) and it takes input from a MCP tool call. These are two fundamentally different functions, so this PR separates them. No functional changes expected.
Test Plan
Test Result
This passes
pytest -sv

vllm.title: [XPU] Support Triton path for LoRA operations on XPU
vllm.author: faaany
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Previously, the XPU platform only supported the native XPU LoRA implementation. This PR adds flexibility to choose between native XPU kernels and Triton-based GPU kernels for LoRA operations on XPU devices.
Additionally, it also fixed the keyword argument launch_pdl bug analog to this PR #28

vllm.title: Add NUMA node validation for CPU thread binding
vllm.author: usberkeley
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Adds validation to ensure all specified CPUs are on the same NUMA node during CPU thread initialization. This prevents potential performance degradation from cross-NUMA node memory access.
Users can specify CPU IDs, which may result in incorrect configuration of CPU IDs across NUMA nodes wit
